question_text: Two forces act on an object: Force A = 50 N to the right and Force B = 30 N to the left. A third force C is then applied. After all three forces act, the object moves at constant velocity in a straight line. What must be true about Force C?

options.label: A
options.text: Force C must be 20 N to the right

options.label: B
options.text: Force C must be 20 N to the left

options.label: C
options.text: Force C can be any magnitude as long as it points left

options.label: D
options.text: Force C is not needed because the object already moves at constant velocity

explanation: For constant velocity motion, all forces must be balanced (resultant = 0). The resultant of A and B is 50 - 30 = 20 N to the right. Force C must exactly cancel this by being 20 N to the left.
